title: Cadence (CDNS) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Cadence Design Systems compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 12% FCF growth rate is justified by the massive tailwinds in custom silicon and AI design, alongside margin expansion inherent to their software model.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8% discount rate is utilized, reflecting the highly predictable, recurring revenue streams and mission-critical nature of EDA software.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate acknowledges that semiconductor innovation is a permanent, foundational element of global technological progress.

thesisTitle: The Architect of the AI Era
thesisParagraphs: Cadence is an essential tollbooth for the semiconductor industry. Without EDA software, modern processors, smartphones, and AI accelerators cannot be built. This positioning grants the company incredible pricing power and near-monopoly characteristics within a duopolistic market structure. The current AI infrastructure build-out is significantly accelerating chip complexity, which directly translates to increased demand for Cadence's most advanced verification and simulation tools.

Despite these incredibly strong fundamentals, the market has priced perfection into the stock. At current levels, CDNS trades at a massive premium that fully accounts for the next decade of hyper-growth. While it is a phenomenal business, the current valuation provides a negative margin of safety, making it vulnerable to any deceleration in tech spending or geopolitical shocks.

faq.question: Why is Cadence considered overvalued despite strong growth?
faq.answer: The market has already priced in an exceptionally optimistic growth scenario for the next decade. Even with a 12% FCF growth rate assumption, the current price exceeds the calculated intrinsic value.

faq.question: Who is Cadence's main competitor?
faq.answer: Synopsys (SNPS) is the other major player in the EDA market, effectively forming a duopoly with Cadence.

faq.question: How does AI affect Cadence?
faq.answer: AI is a massive tailwind. AI chips are incredibly complex, requiring more sophisticated and expensive EDA software to design and verify, directly benefiting Cadence.
